responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking me to send the "security box" (credential input widget) for the GitHub Token. They want to provide a new GitHub token so we can finish the deployment and audit. Let me request the GitHub token via the requestEnvs tool.

Looking at the private environment variables, there's already a GITHUB_TOKEN variable but it has been returning 401. The user wants to provide a fresh one.

Let me also check if there are other env vars needed - but the user specifically asked for the GitHub token only.

Let me also read the current env state to understand what's there.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub personal access token (classic) with repo and workflow scopes. Create at https://github.com/settings/tokens

input.reason: Need a fresh GitHub token to push code and trigger deployment. The current token returns 401 (expired/revoked).

requestedEnvs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requestedEnvs.description: GitHub personal access token (classic) with repo and workflow scopes. Create at https://github.com/settings/tokens

output.reason: Need a fresh GitHub token to push code and trigger deployment. The current token returns 401 (expired/revoked).

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"envs": [{"name": "GITHUB_TOKEN", "description": "GitHub personal access token (classic) with repo and workflow scopes. Create at https://github.com/settings/tokens", "placeholder": "ghp_... or github_pat_...", "isPublic": false}], "reason": "Need a fresh GitHub token to push code and trigger deployment. The current token returns 401 (expired/revoked)."}
